rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A Nelson Co Inc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Asbestos Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 12, 2017, line 2. CENTURY INDEMNITY COMPANY'S Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A Nelson Co Inc. Specially appearing Century Indemnity Company's ("Century") motion for an order setting aside default and default judgment entered against James A. Nelson, Co., Inc. is granted. Century is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant James A. Nelson Co., Inc. on February 19, 2013 and the default judgment entered against defendant James A. Nelson Co., Inc. on January 28, 2014 are set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Relief is not authorized pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(b) because the motion was filed more than six months after both entry of default and default judgment. Relief is not authorized pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(d) because the evidence demonstrates proper service with a summons on Nelson pursuant to C.C.P. § 415.20(a), making the default and default judgment entered against it valid. Plaintiffs' requests for relief pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(c)(1)(A) and 473(c)(1)(C) are denied as the Court is not granting relief based on any of the provisions in section 473. (C.C.P. § 473(c)(1).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Motion For Summary Adjudication [Being Brought On A Shortened Notice Pursuant To Stipulation And Order]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Asbestos Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 12, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT SUPERIOR BOILER WORKS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Motion For Summary Adjudication. Defendant Superior Boiler Works, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and/or motion for summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party's request. =(503/CML)

Set for hearing on the Asbestos Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 12, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rule 2.7(B) for failure to lodge with the Court courtesy copies of defendant's moving papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
